gene_description: Low-Density Lipoprotein Receptor — membrane receptor that mediates hepatic uptake of LDL cholesterol from circulation. Variants in the promoter/intron-1 region modulate receptor expression and LDL-C levels.

source.title: Identification of the Functional Variant(s) that Explain the Low-Density Lipoprotein Receptor (LDLR) GWAS SNP rs6511720 Association with Lower LDL-C and Risk of CHD
source.finding: LDLR rs6511720 T allele causes 29% increased LDLR expression via enhanced transcription factor binding, leading to lower LDL-C (effect size -0.15 to -0.26 mmol/L) and reduced coronary heart disease risk.

GG.effect: Homozygous major allele carriers have baseline LDLR expression levels. Higher LDL-C levels compared to T allele carriers. Standard coronary heart disease risk from this locus. Represents the majority genotype (~81% in Europeans).
GG.recommendation: Standard lipid management per clinical guidelines. Heart-healthy diet with focus on reducing saturated fat and increasing soluble fiber. Statin therapy if LDL-C elevated per risk assessment.

GT.effect: Heterozygous T carriers have ~15% higher LDLR expression. LDL-C reduced by approximately 0.15-0.26 mmol/L (6-10 mg/dL). Modest reduction in coronary heart disease and myocardial infarction risk.
GT.recommendation: Favorable genetic profile for LDL-C. Maintain heart-healthy lifestyle. May respond particularly well to statins. Other cardiovascular risk factors still apply.

TG.effect: Heterozygous T carriers have ~15% higher LDLR expression. LDL-C reduced by approximately 0.15-0.26 mmol/L (6-10 mg/dL). Modest reduction in coronary heart disease and myocardial infarction risk.
TG.recommendation: Favorable genetic profile for LDL-C. Maintain heart-healthy lifestyle. May respond particularly well to statins. Other cardiovascular risk factors still apply.

TT.effect: Homozygous minor allele carriers (rare, ~1%) have ~29% increased LDLR expression. Significantly lower LDL-C levels. Reduced risk of coronary heart disease and abdominal aortic aneurysm. Naturally efficient hepatic LDL clearance.
TT.recommendation: Highly favorable lipid genetics. Standard healthy lifestyle. Lowest risk from this locus.
